subject: Anthropology
title: Metaphor and Thought
description: A quick win: understand Metaphor and Thought.

hook.text: Time is money. Life is a journey. Arguments are wars. You think in metaphors without even realizing it.

buildup.text: Lakoff and Johnson argued that metaphors aren't just poetic — they structure everyday thought and reasoning.

discovery.text: We understand abstract concepts through concrete ones: time as space, ideas as objects, emotions as forces.

twist.text: Different cultures use different metaphors. In Mandarin, time flows vertically. In English, it flows horizontally.

climax.text: Metaphors reveal hidden frameworks. The metaphors a culture uses shape what its people can easily think and say.

punchline.text: Metaphors are invisible maps of thought.

quiz.question: What did Lakoff and Johnson argue about metaphors?
options: They are only used in poetry
They structure everyday thought and reasoning
